def solve(landStartTime, landDuration, waterStartTime, waterDuration)
  n = landStartTime.length
  m = waterStartTime.length

  # Calculate all possible end times for land and water attractions
  land_end_times = []
  water_end_times = []

  # For each land attraction, calculate the end time if it's taken first
  land_start_times = landStartTime
  land_durations = landDuration
  land_start_times.each_with_index do |start, i|
    land_end_times << [start + land_durations[i], i]
  end

  # For each water attraction, calculate the end time if it's taken first
  water_start_times = waterStartTime
  water_durations = waterDuration
  water_start_times.each_with_index do |start, j|
    water_end_times << [start + water_durations[j], j]
  end

  # Sort the end times for land and water attractions
  land_end_times.sort!
  water_end_times.sort!

  # Check all possible combinations of land and water attractions
  min_time = Float::INFINITY

  # Try all combinations of land and water attractions
  land_end_times.each do |land_end|
    land_time, land_idx = land_end
    # Find the earliest water attraction that can be taken after the land attraction
    water_end_times.each do |water_end|
      water_time, water_idx = water_end
      # The water attraction can be taken after the land attraction if:
      # water_start_time <= land_end_time and water_start_time <= water_end_time
      # or water_start_time <= land_end_time and water_start_time <= water_end_time
      # We need to check if the water attraction can be taken after the land attraction
      # If the water attraction starts after the land ends, we can take it immediately
      if water_start_times[water_idx] <= land_end_time
        # The water attraction can be taken after the land attraction
        # The end time is max(land_end_time, water_start_times[water_idx]) + water_duration
        start_time = [land_end_time, water_start_times[water_idx]].max
        end_time = start_time + water_durations[water_idx]
        min_time = [min_time, end_time].min
      end
    end
  end

  # Try all combinations of water and land attractions
  water_end_times.each do |water_end|
    water_time, water_idx = water_end
    # Find the earliest land attraction that can be taken after the water attraction
    land_end_times.each do |land_end|
      land_time, land_idx = land_end
      # The land attraction can be taken after the water attraction if:
      # land_start_time <= water_end_time and land_start_time <= land_end_time
      # or land_start_time <= water_end_time and land_start_time <= land_end_time
      if land_start_times[land_idx] <= water_end_time
        # The land attraction can be taken after the water attraction
        # The end time is max(water_end_time, land_start_times[land_idx]) + land_duration
        start_time = [water_end_time, land_start_times[land_idx]].max
        end_time = start_time + land_durations[land_idx]
        min_time = [min_time, end_time].min
      end
    end
  end

  min_time
end
